channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Alisson é cortado, e Ancelotti convoca Hugo Souza, do Corinthians
item.description: The CBF called up Corinthians goalkeeper Hugo Souza to replace the injured Alisson for Brazil's friendlies against France and Croatia in March 2026. Ancelotti maintained that the goalkeeper position remains open among Hugo Souza, Bento, and Ederson, stating no spot is 100% secured ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: TSE confirma multa de R$ 15 mil a Pablo Marçal por chamar Nunes de 'canalha'
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) unanimously upheld the conviction of Pablo Marçal for abusive electoral propaganda after he called São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes a 'scoundrel' and 'coward' in a video. The R$15,000 fine was confirmed, rejecting Marçal's appeal.

item.title: Sônia Guajajara deixará ministério para disputar reeleição como deputada federal por SP
item.description: Minister of Indigenous Peoples Sônia Guajajara will leave office on March 30 to run for re-election as a federal deputy from São Paulo. She highlighted her administration’s legacy, including the resumption of indigenous land demarcation and bringing Indigenous issues to the center of public debate. Eloy Terena, the ministry’s executive secretary, is a possible successor.

item.title: Flávio diz que apoiou Moro ao governo do PR após saber que Ratinho Jr. será seu adversário
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) announced his support for Sérgio Moro's (União-PR) pre-candidacy for governor of Paraná after learning that current governor Ratinho Jr. (PSD-PR) would be Moro's opponent. He stated the decision was based on the need to unite forces against the current state government.

item.title: De saída da prefeitura, Paes critica Castro e Witzel e agradece a Lula
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes handed over office to his deputy Eduardo Cavaliere and announced his pre-candidacy for state governor. During the ceremony, he criticized current governor Cláudio Castro and former governor Wilson Witzel, while thanking President Lula for his support. Paes positioned himself as the leader of the opposition to the current state government, reinforcing his alliance with the national PT.

item.title: Aliados de Boulos acusam militantes de dividir partido e apontam fogo amigo no PSOL
item.description: Allies of Guilherme Boulos accuse dissident militants of PSOL of engaging in 'friendly fire' by releasing a note claiming the minister planned to leave the party for the PT, using the federation proposal as a pretext. Boulos' faction, Revolução Solidária, denies the allegation, claims there has been internal persecution since he joined Lula's government, and criticizes party sectors that prefer keeping PSOL as a 'critical conscience of the left' without seeking institutional power. Boulos denies an immediate party change but admits the topic is under discussion, confirming early talks with the PT.

item.title: Eduardo Paes renuncia à Prefeitura do Rio para disputar governo do estado
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes (PSD) resigned to run for state governor in 2026 after 4,827 days in office. His decision, supported by President Lula (PT), complies with legal requirements for candidacy. Deputy Mayor Eduardo Cavaliere (PSD) assumes the mayoralty, pledging continuity in governance.

item.title: O anúncio de Arthur Lira que reforça a difícil vida de Lula em Alagoas
item.description: Arthur Lira announced his pre-candidacy for the Senate from Alagoas in 2026, emphasizing the need for a senator who works in Brasília and indirectly criticizing Senator Renan Calheiros. The announcement reignites the historic rivalry between Lira and Calheiros, which poses a challenge for Lula’s political coordination in the state, given both figures’ ties to the PT government. Lira’s bid is backed by Bolsonaro’s PL, while the absence of Maceió’s mayor JHC (PL) at the event drew attention despite the party’s support for his candidacy.

item.title: Missão Apoio
item.description: Former minister and governor Moreira Franco launched a memoir exceeding one thousand pages, criticizing the 'salad' of alliances within the MDB and the party's current leadership. The book reveals behind-the-scenes details of the political crisis and affirms his support for Missão Apoio. It was published on March 15, 2026.

item.title: Arthur Lira lança pré-candidatura ao Senado por Alagoas em evento com lideranças municipais
item.description: Arthur Lira launched his pre-candidacy for the Senate from Alagoas at an event with municipal leaders, emphasizing his congressional tenure and his father’s legacy. He announced a strong slate for the Federal Chamber, including his 20-year-old son Álvaro Lira, and stressed dialogue with local leaders despite the absence of Maceió’s mayor, JHC.

item.title: Lula critica privatizações e diz que Brasil é dono da Petrobras
item.description: President Lula criticized privatizations, particularly of BR Distribuidora (now Vibra Energia), during an event at the Gabriel Passos Refinery in Betim, MG, while announcing R$9 billion in Petrobras investments. He emphasized that Brazil owns Petrobras and defended the state company's strategic role, linking current difficulties to past privatizations.

item.title: Governador do Ceará confirma presença no VEJA Fórum de Infraestrutura
item.description: Ceará Governor Elmano de Freitas and Transport Ministry Executive Secretary George Santoro confirmed participation in the VEJA Infrastructure Forum, scheduled for February 27 in São Paulo. The event will feature São Paulo Governor Tarcísio Gomes de Freitas and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president, alongside business leaders and experts to discuss infrastructure investment and public-private partnerships.

item.title: André Mendonça é o ministro mais bem avaliado do STF
item.description: André Mendonça is the best-rated STF minister with 43% positive image, while Dias Toffoli, Gilmar Mendes, Alexandre de Moraes and Flávio Dino have the worst evaluations, with up to 81% rejection. Trust in the Court dropped from 48.5% to 34% in eight months, and 59.5% of Brazilians believe most ministers do not demonstrate competence and impartiality.

item.title: 'Estou centrado no Ceará, mas me coçando de olho no Brasil', diz Ciro Gomes
item.description: Former governor and minister Ciro Gomes, recently affiliated with the PSDB and a pre-candidate in Ceará, said he is focused on the state election but is eyeing a national political role. He stated that while the situation in Ceará is consolidated, he is 'itching to look at Brazil,' signaling ambitions beyond the state level.

item.title: Sem BR Distribuidora, guerra no Irã encare combustíveis, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula blamed the privatization of BR Distribuidora and the U.S.-Iran tensions for rising fuel prices in Brazil, arguing that without a state-owned distributor, the government cannot prevent Petrobras price increases from fully reaching consumers. He announced R$9 billion in Petrobras investments and said the government issued a provisional measure to zero federal diesel taxes and create subsidies to offset recent price hikes. The PT is now mobilizing in Congress to re-establish BR Distribuidora as a state entity, despite a legal non-compete clause blocking Petrobras from returning to retail until 2029.

item.title: Paes transmite cargo a vice e entra na disputa pelo governo do Rio
item.description: Eduardo Paes (PSD) resigned as mayor of Rio de Janeiro to run for a third time for state governor, transferring power to his deputy, Eduardo Cavaliere. Paes leads in voter intention polls and has the support of President Lula, having recently clashed with Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) over security and the politicization of state police.

item.title: O cálculo de Lula em São Paulo diante do favoritismo de Tarcísio
item.description: Lula launched Fernando Haddad as the pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo and pressured Geraldo Alckmin to run for Senate, aiming to strengthen the PT's ticket in 2026 amid Tarcísio de Freitas' favoritism. The strategy seeks to secure a competitive platform in the country's largest electoral college, but faces internal resistance and the PT's historical weakness in São Paulo, alongside issues like security and Cracolândia that favor the right.

item.title: Lula diz que Brasil é dono da Petrobras e critica privatização da BR em meio à alta do petróleo
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that Petrobras is a national asset and criticized the past privatization of BR Distribuidora, saying it hindered government efforts to control diesel prices amid rising oil prices. He emphasized the importance of the state-owned company in fuel pricing policy.

item.title: Lira lança pré-candidatura ao Senado com recados a JHC e a Renan
item.description: Arthur Lila launched his pre-candidacy for the Senate from Alagoas, emphasizing the importance of political word and implicitly criticizing adversaries without naming them. He will directly confront Renan Calheiros in the Senate race, while Maceió’s mayor João Henrique Cladas (JHC) was absent from the event, signaling a breakdown in their alliance. JHC is being courted by the MDB to run alongside Renan Filho for governor but has not yet decided between running for Senate or state governor.

item.title: Ouvidoria em Foco: TSE busca criar rede de enfrentamento da violência de gênero
item.description: The TSE's Women's Ombudsman received 25 gender-based violence complaints between January and February 2026, including political violence, harassment, and discrimination. To address this, the TSE will hold workshops in March and April focused on identifying and forwarding complaints, aiming to ensure full exercise of women's citizenship.

item.title: Ministro mais apagado, diz economista sobre Dario Durigan
item.description: The appointment of Dario Durigan as Minister of Finance is seen as a technical and discreet choice, unlikely to significantly impact markets, according to economist Bruno Perri. He is expected to act as a guarantor of the current economic agenda, ensuring continuity rather than driving structural changes, and his tenure is viewed as temporary — aligned with a political transition until Lula seeks a minister with stronger political negotiation skills.

item.title: Ministério Público denuncia assessor de Marçal por fake news contra Boulos
item.description: The Federal Public Prosecutor's Office (MPF) has filed charges against Rafael Nogueira, an advisor to Pablo Marçal in the 2024 municipal campaign, for spreading false videos linking Minister Guilherme Boulos to illicit drug use. The action aims to combat electoral disinformation and hold accountable those involved in disseminating fake news during campaigns. The case highlights the use of misinformation as a political strategy in recent electoral contests.

item.title: Clica e Confirma destaca Portal de Dados Abertos e campanha Mesário Voluntário 2026
item.description: The Clica e Confirma program highlights the TSE's Open Data Portal, which provides public access to hundreds of datasets on Brazilian elections, including candidacies, voter registrations, results, and party finances. It also covers the national 'Volunteer Poll Worker 2026' campaign, encouraging citizens to sign up for election day duties on October 4 and 25, 2026. Additionally, it notes the swearing-in of new TSE substitute minister Nauê Bernardo Pinheiro de Azevedo, who will serve until March 2028.

item.title: Arthur Lira anuncia pré-candidatura ao Senado por Alagoas
item.description: Federal Deputy Arthur Lira (PP-AL) announced his pre-candidacy for the Senate from Alagoas in the October 2026 elections during an event at Hotel Ritz Lagoa da Anta in Maceió. He emphasized his health-related achievements in the state as his main legacy across four terms in the Chamber of Deputies and reiterated that politics is the path to transform Alagoas.

item.title: É #FAKE que vídeo de Bolsonaro deitado em leito de UTI seja recente; imagem foi registrada após facada em 2018
item.description: The video circulating on social media showing Jair Bolsonaro in an ICU, claimed to be from a recent hospitalization, is false. The footage was recorded in 2018 after the stabbing attack during his presidential campaign, at Albert Einstein Hospital in São Paulo. Michelle Bolsonaro confirmed the video’s date, and reverse image searches verified its prior online use, debunking the claim of a current hospitalization.

item.title: Escândalo Master já impacta os números de Lula, avalia diretor do Paraná Pesquisas
item.description: The Banco Master scandal is damaging President Lula’s image, as voters tend to blame the government in power for corruption crises, according to Paraná Pesquisas director Murilo Hidalgo. Despite Lula’s attempt to link the scandal to the Bolsonaro administration, the fallout is primarily affecting his government, worsening institutional wear and bringing corruption back to the forefront of the 2026 election. This trend is expected to make the campaign more negative and personality-driven, reducing space for policy debate and alternatives outside the Lula-Bolsonaro polarization.

item.title: Lula falava sobre Ozempic ao recomendar andar a pé, e não sobre combustível
item.description: President Lula (PT) promoted walking and physical activity while discussing the use of Ozempic for diabetes and obesity treatment, not suggesting people walk due to high fuel costs. Misleading posts edit his remarks to omit context, falsely claiming he linked the recommendation to rising gasoline prices. Lula stressed that the drug should not be a 'gift' for those who avoid exercise or unhealthy eating, emphasizing that physical activity and medical guidance are necessary.

item.title: Veja quem são os vices que assumem com a saída de Paes, João Campos e Zema
item.description: Mayors Eduardo Paes (PSD) of Rio de Janeiro and João Campos (PSB) of Recife are expected to resign from their positions in the coming days to run for state governor in Rio and Pernambuco in October 2026. Their respective vice mayors will assume the mayoral offices until the end of the current terms.

item.title: Boulos nega saída do Psol para se filiar ao PT
item.description: Minister Guilherme Boulos of the Psol party denied rumors that he would leave the party to join the PT, expressing regret that part of the party had disseminated a 'forged letter' suggesting his departure. He stated that the proposal for a federation with the PT was rejected by the Psol’s National Directorate on March 7, despite being supported by his faction, Revolutionary Solidarity. Boulos reaffirmed his commitment to the Psol, which he has been affiliated with since 2018, and accused the dissent of acting with opportunism and desperation.

item.title: Crusoé: Não é sobre Bolsonaro. É sobre quem julga
item.description: The debate over taking former President Jair Bolsonaro’s case to an international tribunal is often framed as another episode of Brazil’s polarization, but the real issue is deeper: it concerns the legitimacy of the judgment process itself. The doubt is not only about the outcome, but about the credibility and impartiality of the tribunal conducting it. The core question is, therefore, who has the authority to judge and under what criteria.

item.title: Master estraçalha imagem de STF e corte prova do remédio amargo que já afetou Forças Armadas
item.description: The scandal involving banker Daniel Vorcaro, his ties to STF ministers like Dias Toffoli and Alexandre de Moraes, and his plea deal are eroding public trust in the Supreme Federal Court, as shown by AtlasIntel research. This loss of credibility mirrors the experience of the Armed Forces after January 8, when their links to Bolsonaro and support for the coup attempt damaged their institutional image. Both cases illustrate how controversial political alliances can cause lasting harm to the reputation of traditionally respected institutions.

item.title: Caso Banco Master é conhecido por mais de 97% dos brasileiros, mostra AtlasIntel
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll shows that 97.6% of Brazilians are aware of the Banco Master scandal, with 74.7% claiming detailed knowledge and 22.9% having only heard about it. The case, involving the bank's extrajudicial liquidation in 2025 over suspected financial fraud, has sparked broad political repercussions, including alleged ties between controller Daniel Vorcaro and figures from PP, União Brasil, PT, and even President Lula, as well as payments to law firms linked to former ministers.

item.title: Sorteio da Libertadores reforça favoritismo dos brasileiros nas odds
item.description: The 2026 Libertadores draw confirmed Brazilian clubs as favorites, with Flamengo leading the odds at 4.00, followed by Palmeiras (6.00), Fluminense and Corinthians (11.00 each), and Cruzeiro (13.00). Since 2019, only Brazilian teams have won the tournament, and Brazil now has 25 titles, matching Argentina. Boca Juniors is the first non-Brazilian team in the favorites list, with odds of 12.00.

item.title: Kassab escolhe Ratinho para disputar Presidência pelo PSD, diz conselheiro
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab is set to nominate Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. as the party's presidential candidate for 2026, following consultation with a veteran council led by Jorge Bornhausen. The decision was unanimous among tested PSD governors, citing Ratinho’s center-right profile and broad electoral appeal across social strata. Official confirmation is expected between March 25 and 27, before the deadline for candidates to resign from current offices.

item.title: Os taxistas de Cármen Lúcia tinham razão: desconfiança no STF rompe a polarização pela primeira vez
item.description: Trust in the Supreme Federal Court (STF) dropped to 34% while distrust rose to 60%, breaking for the first time the barrier of political polarization, according to AtlasIntel. Even among Lula voters, one in four now distrusts the Court, showing the legitimacy crisis transcends ideological lines. Minister Cármen Lúcia noted that, unlike in 2015, even taxi drivers now criticize the STF, reflecting a broad erosion of confidence following the Master case revelations.

item.title: João Campos lança pré-candidatura ao governo de Pernambuco
item.description: Recife Mayor João Campos (PSB) officially launched his pre-candidacy for governor of Pernambuco in the 2026 elections. The event also confirmed the pre-candidacy of Marília Arraes (PDT) for the Senate, forming a progressive ticket. The ceremony took place at the Luzeiros Hotel in the state capital.

item.title: Grupo de Boulos no PSOL afirma que foi informado que ele deixará o partido e vai ao PT
item.description: The dissident group Operativo Nacional da Dissidência da Revolução Solidária (ONDRS) claims Guilherme Boulos has decided to leave PSOL and join the PT, with negotiations beginning in December last year, including arrangements for his wife, Natália Boulos, to run for office under the PT. Boulos attended the launch of Fernando Haddad’s pre-candidacy for São Paulo governor alongside PT leaders, raising concerns that his departure could take hundreds of militants and parliamentarians like Erika Hilton, though he has not officially denied it, stating only that they are 'internally discussing their political paths'.

item.title: Uma nova pedra no caminho de Flávio Bolsonaro rumo ao embate contra Lula
item.description: Senator Tereza Cristina declined to run as Flávio Bolsonaro’s vice-presidential candidate, choosing to retain her Senate seat from Mato Grosso do Sul. Her refusal is a strategic setback for the PL, which viewed her as a key figure to balance the ticket with her technical, moderate profile and appeal to agribusiness and female voters. The party must now restart its search for a female running mate capable of reducing rejection among women and broadening economic outreach.

item.title: Ex-assessora de Flávio é denunciada por suspeita de lavar dinheiro
item.description: The former aide to Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, Raimunda Veras Magalhães, was denounced by the Rio de Janeiro Public Ministry for suspected money laundering linked to Adriano da Nóbrega’s illegal gambling operation, the Escritório do Crime. Prosecutors allege she helped operate front companies that moved approximately R$ 8.5 million between 2016 and 2018, during which time she earned a salary of R$ 6,490.35 while working for Flávio Bolsonaro, then a state deputy.

item.title: Sem JHC, Lira se lança ao Senado e confirma o filho na disputa pela Câmara
item.description: Federal Deputy Arthur Lira (PP-AL) launched his pre-candidacy for the Senate from Alagoas, confirming that his son Álvaro Lira, 20, will run for a seat in the Chamber of Deputies, aided by a legal change that delays the age verification for office. The event took place without the presence of Maceió's mayor, JHC (PL), with whom Lira had a political agreement that deteriorated due to disagreements over alliances and judicial appointments.

item.title: Quem Carlos Bolsonaro chamou de “bumbum guloso” em post enigmático
item.description: Carlos Bolsonaro posted an enigmatic message on X targeting former prosecutor Deltan Dallagnol, calling him an 'engraved vagabond' and 'greedy buttocks', referencing the 'dutch rowing' meme used to mock moderate right-wing figures. He accused Dallagnol and groups like Novo of exploiting Bolsonarism for electoral gain, while defending the grassroots support of his brother Flávio Bolsonaro as the foundation of their political success.

item.title: Vamos comprar refinaria vendida pela Petrobras, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula stated that the government intends to repurchase the Landulpho Alves Refinery, sold by Petrobras in 2021 during Bolsonaro's administration. The move is supported by Minister Alexandre Silveira and backed by CGU findings that the sale occurred below market value during the COVID-19 pandemic. The refinery was purchased by Abu Dhabi's Mubadala fund for US$1.65 billion.

item.title: Lula defende estoque de petróleo para o "país não ficar chupando dedo"
item.description: President Lula advocated for a strategic fuel reserve and a state-owned distributor within Petrobras to protect consumers during global crises. He criticized the privatization of BR Distribuidora, stating that consumers currently 'suck their thumb' while distributors profit from price adjustments set by Petrobras. Lula emphasized that the absence of state control over distribution limits the government's ability to lower prices during market speculation, disproportionately affecting the poor.

item.title: Zema deixa governo de MG no domingo, e Mateus Simões toma posse
item.description: Romeu Zema (Novo) resigns as governor of Minas Gerais this Sunday, with his vice, Mateus Simões (PSD), set to assume office at 10 a.m. in the ALMG. The ceremony will be led by state deputy Tadeu Leite (MDB), followed by a formal handover at the Palácio da Liberdade. Zema is considered a potential presidential candidate for Novo, while Simões has suggested that a partnership with Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) could unify the right in Minas and boost his electoral prospects in October.

item.title: Luciano Hang coloca peruca loira para criticar Erika Hilton
item.description: Luciano Hang, owner of Havan, posted a video on X wearing a blonde wig and women's clothing to criticize Erika Hilton's (Psol-SP) election as president of the Women's Rights Defense Commission, questioning her legitimacy and citing an 84% disagreement rate. He compared his act to that of Deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG), who in 2023 wore a wig during International Women's Day and was later ordered to pay R$200,000 in collective moral damages. Governists defended Hilton, while Hang and Ferreira's allies reinforced transphobic criticisms of her leadership.

item.title: EUA investigam Petro por suposta ligação com traficantes de drogas
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro is under criminal investigation by two U.S. federal prosecutors’ offices in New York over alleged ties to drug traffickers and possible solicitation of illegal donations during his presidential campaign. The investigations, led by narcotics specialists and involving the DEA, are in early stages and have not yet led to charges. While there is no evidence the White House initiated the probes, Donald Trump has used the case to pressure Colombia and potentially influence the country’s May 2026 presidential election.

item.title: O calendário dos sonhos do relator do PL de regulamentação de trabalho por apps
item.description: The rapporteur of the bill regulating app-based work, Deputy Augusto Coutinho (Republicans), set a timeline to finalize the text by late March and vote it in committee or plenary by early April. The main disagreement is the minimum payment per delivery: R$ 8.50 proposed by the rapporteur versus R$ 10 plus R$ 2.50 per additional kilometer advocated by the government. Despite concerns about negative electoral impact in 2026, the government still views the proposal as a priority.

item.title: Crusoé: De faccionados para terroristas
item.description: The Brazilian federal government, through Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira, opposes the U.S. proposal to designate the First Capital Command (PCC) and the Red Command (CV) as Foreign Terrorist Organizations (FTOs). Vieira argues that such a move could harm international cooperation against organized crime without addressing its root causes. The potential designation is noted as a factor that could influence the 2026 presidential election landscape.

item.title: Entra em vigor lei que amplia garantias para financiamentos do Pronaf
item.description: Law 13.356/26, signed by President Lula, authorizes the Guarantee Fund for Operations (FGO) to guarantee Pronaf credit operations, unlocking up to R$500 million in uncommitted funds to expand credit for family farming. The measure eliminates the pecuniary commission, reduces risk for banks, and facilitates access to loans for production costs, equipment, and property investment — especially for small producers lacking traditional collateral.

item.title: Em novo aceno a policiais no ano eleitoral, Tarcísio pagará bônus por desempenho
item.description: The São Paulo government announced a R$ 609 million performance bonus (BR) for police officers for 2025, to be paid in April. Governor Tarcísio de Freitas said the payment recognizes police dedication and performance, tied to SSP-defined indicators, and strengthens public safety. The move comes amid pressure for wage increases, as the governor also submitted four bills to the state legislature proposing around a 10% salary raise for civil and military police.
